task_completion.description: GeoGebra is opened with the 3D Graphics view active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GeoGebra application window is visible, showing a 3D coordinate system with x, y, and z axes.

task_completion.description: Vectors u = (1, 2, -2) and v = (3, 0, 1) are entered and plotted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Algebra View sidebar shows the definitions for vectors u=(1, 2, -2) and v=(3, 0, 1), and they are visible as arrows in the 3D view.

task_completion.description: The cross product vector is calculated and plotted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Algebra View shows a new vector resulting from the cross product, with coordinates (2, -7, -6), and it is visible as an arrow in the 3D view.

task_completion.description: A parallelogram spanned by vectors u and v is created in the 3D view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A shaded polygon (parallelogram) is visible in the 3D view, with its adjacent sides formed by vectors u and v.

task_completion.description: A text annotation showing the cross product result or its magnitude/area is added to the visualization.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text box is visible in the GeoGebra graphics area containing either the vector coordinates '(2, -7, -6)' or the area/magnitude value (approximately '9.43' or '9.434').

task_completion.description: The project is saved as cross_product_3d.ggb in ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save' dialog box shows the filename 'cross_product_3d.ggb' and the target directory, OR the GeoGebra window title updates to 'cross_product_3d.ggb', OR a terminal/file manager shows the file in the correct path.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the GeoGebra GUI rather than generating the file via a script.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GeoGebra interface being actively used (e.g., typing in the input bar, using geometry tools, clicking menus).

integrity.description: The visualization is genuinely created in GeoGebra, not an image editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard GeoGebra interface elements (Algebra View, 3D Graphics View, toolbars) are present and consistent with the plotted objects.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply write the answers into a text file instead of creating the visualization.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final output is a GeoGebra project file (.ggb) and a visual 3D plot, not just a text document containing the numbers.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Vector u is (1, 2, -2). Vector v is (3, 0, 1). The expected cross product vector is (2, -7, -6). The expected area/magnitude of the parallelogram is approximately 9.434 (or 9.43). The file must be saved as 'cross_product_3d.ggb'.
